title: Storeman
description:
Company Overview

BT Airworks deals with the assembly, maintenance and repair of Unit Load Devices (containers, pellets, nets) and Galley carts and trolleys.

Job Summary

You will manage the receipt, storage, issuance, and inventory control of parts, tools, and consumables to support aerospace maintenance operations, ensuring compliance with industry standards and safety regulations.

Responsibilities

  • Receive, verify, and inspect incoming parts, tools, and consumables against delivery orders and specifications to ensure accuracy and quality
  • Store inventory items properly following aviation industry storage and handling standards to maintain safety and usability
  • Issue materials and parts to technicians according to work orders and maintain accurate transaction records for traceability
  • Organize and secure the store environment to support efficient operations and prevent loss or damage
  • Monitor stock levels regularly and initiate re-ordering to avoid shortages or excess inventory
  • Conduct periodic stock checks and full inventory audits to maintain accurate inventory records
  • Track traceability and shelf-life of aircraft consumables and components to ensure compliance with safety requirements
  • Label and record all incoming and outgoing items accurately in the inventory management system for effective tracking
  • Coordinate with suppliers, freight forwarders, and internal teams to ensure timely availability of materials and parts
  • Manage issuance, calibration tracking, and return of tools and ground support equipment to maintain operational readiness
  • Support regulatory and quality audits by maintaining accurate and up-to-date store documentation
  • Follow health, safety, and environmental regulations within the workshop and store to maintain a safe workplace
  • Assist operations teams with material handling and store-related tasks as needed to support smooth workflow

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • GCE ‘N’ / ‘O’ Levels or equivalent; additional logistics or warehouse training preferred
  • Prior experience in a warehouse or store role, preferably in aerospace or engineering environments
  • Familiarity with aviation industry standards such as CAAS or FAA is an advantage
  • Knowledge of inventory control systems and Microsoft Office applications
  • Physically fit to lift and move heavy items safely as required
  • Good communication, organizational, and record-keeping skills to support accurate documentation and teamwork
  • Strong attention to detail and a safety-first mindset to maintain compliance and prevent errors
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced workshop environment
  • Forklift license is advantageous

Other Information

  • Working Hours: Monday to Friday (8:00 AM - 5:30 PM)
  • Basic Salary: $2,000 + Fixed skill allowance (based on skill set/experience) + Overtime (negotiable)
  • Training: Provided
  • Work Location: Changi South
